name: Salesforce, Inc.

profile.description: Salesforce is the world's largest CRM platform provider and a leader in enterprise AI through Agentforce. Q1 FY2027 revenue of $11.13B (up 13% YoY), Agentforce ARR of $1.2B (up 205% YoY), and 34.8% non-GAAP operating margin. 83,334 employees.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): Agentforce ARR reached $1.2B (up 205% YoY) with 3.8B agentic work units delivered, growing 111% QoQ [1]. Revenue of $11.13B up 13% YoY with subscription revenue up 14%. This represents the most significant product transformation since the cloud platform launch. AI Deployment (2/2): Agentforce and Data 360 combined ARR reached ~$3.4B (up 200%+ YoY). Non-GAAP EPS of $3.88 up 50% YoY [1]. Market Share (2/2): Dominant global CRM platform with expanding enterprise AI market share through Agentforce. Product Pipeline (1/2): Data 360 ingested 52 trillion records (up 136% YoY), Agentforce product expansion ongoing [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Massive AI R&D investment.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): 'Bringing companies and customers together' has been validated by 25 years of CRM innovation. Community Impact (1/2): 1-1-1 philanthropic model (1% equity, time, product to nonprofits) is distinctive. Employee Retention (0/2): 83,334 employees after layoffs. Stock down 33% in 2026 despite earnings beats. Insider Buying (1/2): Some executive purchases.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. Revenue of $11.13B up 13% with 34.8% non-GAAP operating margin and EPS of $3.88 (up 50%) [1]. Forward P/E of 12.15 and PEG of 0.98 represent excellent value for enterprise software. ROE of 16.91% exceeds target. D/E of 124.28% reflects past acquisitions. Profit margin of 18.73% approaching the 20% target.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (5/9): CRM software faces meaningful AI disruption — AI agents could automate sales processes that Salesforce platforms facilitate, and AI-native CRM competitors could emerge [1]. However, Salesforce's enterprise relationships and data moat provide defense. AI Opportunity (1/1): Agentforce at $1.2B ARR growing 205% demonstrates massive AI revenue [1].

infinite_game.summary: Salesforce under Marc Benioff demonstrates strong infinite game characteristics — the 1-1-1 model, consistent mission, and willingness to cannibalize existing products for AI-first approaches show long-term thinking. However, the stock's 33% decline despite earnings beats suggests market skepticism about the AI transition.

investment_thesis: Salesforce's Agentforce ARR of $1.2B (up 205% YoY) represents the most significant AI monetization in enterprise software [1]. At 12.15x forward earnings with PEG of 0.98, CRM offers exceptional value for a 13% revenue grower with 34.8% non-GAAP margins and expanding AI capabilities.
thesis_continued: CRM stock declined 33% in 2026 despite earnings beats, reflecting market concern that AI could disrupt the traditional CRM model that generates Salesforce's core revenue [1]. D/E of 124.28% limits acquisition capacity. The question is whether Agentforce creates net new revenue or cannibalizes existing subscriptions.
key_catalysts: Agentforce ARR accelerating beyond $1.2B as enterprise AI agent adoption reaches critical mass
Non-GAAP margin expansion toward 40% as AI-driven efficiency reduces sales and delivery costs
Data 360 platform becoming the enterprise data backbone for AI deployments
key_risks: AI-native CRM competitors potentially offering simpler, cheaper alternatives that bypass traditional Salesforce platform complexity
Agentforce cannibalization risk — AI agents may reduce the number of human CRM users, shrinking seat-based revenue
Stock price decline of 33% creating valuation overhang and employee retention challenges
sources: Salesforce Q1 FY2027 Earnings Release — SEC 8-K Filing, May 2026
Yahoo Finance — CRM Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
